title: Behavior changes for continuous jobs and pipelines
body:
In early August 2026, continuous Lakeflow Spark Declarative Pipelines and jobs will receive updates to simplify configuration and state management.

**Continuous pipelines**

You will be able to configure a continuous schedule directly from the pipeline page, including job-level settings such as [performance mode](/aws/en/ldp/serverless#performance). Configuring a continuous schedule this way wraps the pipeline in a continuous job.

Databricks recommends running continuous pipelines with a continuous job rather than the pipeline's built-in continuous setting. When a continuous job wraps a pipeline, the job will manage the pipeline's execution lifecycle. The built-in continuous setting is not being removed, but new continuous pipelines should use the continuous job pattern.

**Continuous jobs**

State management for continuous jobs will be simplified. The following breaking changes will take effect:

* Adding a continuous schedule to a job will cancel any active runs, whether the schedule is paused or unpaused.
* Removing a continuous schedule from a job will cancel any active runs.
* Running a paused continuous job will unpause it.
* Pausing a continuous job will also cancel the active run.
* Stopping the active run of a continuous job will also pause the job.

A new read-only API field will track the state of a continuous job.

Review any automation or workflows that rely on the current continuous job behaviors and update them before this release.
